steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence says scientists "have yet to demonstrate" that primary objects would be similarly affected — even though NASA already succeeded with a secondary object. The blank must signal a contrast between that success and the unproven claim about primary objects.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — though,: Signals contrast — even though the secondary-object deflection worked, scientists still have not proven the same for primary objects. ✓
  • Choice B — for example,: Introduces an illustration, not a contrast. The unproven claim about primary objects is not an example of the successful secondary-object deflection. ✗
  • Choice C — likewise,: Signals similarity — the opposite of what is needed, since scientists have not demonstrated the same result for primary objects. ✗
  • Choice D — moreover,: Adds supporting information, not a contrast. The sentence turns against the prior claim rather than continuing it. ✗
A is correct.
